title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic's Massive IPO Filing
summary: Anthropic files for a massive IPO, Google uses Gemini for I/O 2026, and more AI updates.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Anthropic Confidentially Files for What Could Be the Largest IPO Ever
imageCaption: Anthropic Confidentially Files for What Could Be the Largest IPO Ever
imageSourceTitle: Anthropic Confidentially Files for What Could Be the Largest IPO Ever

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. I've got some big news to share with you today, straight from the world of artificial intelligence. Let's dive right in. We've got a few stories to cover, and I'm excited to break them down for you.

Anthropic, a $965 billion company, has confidentially filed for an initial public offering. This could be one of the largest IPOs ever, and it's a significant move for the AI industry. The filing with US regulators is the first step in what could be a blockbuster debut. I'm curious to see how this will play out, as it could set a new benchmark for AI companies looking to go public.

Google has revealed how they used their Gemini model to build Google I/O 2026. From the jellyfish pre-show to their 'TPU Training Day' film, Gemini helped make the event happen. It's always interesting to see these models in action, and Google's transparency about their use cases is a welcome change of pace.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
